marketState: neutral, leaning cautiously bearish — price sits right at fair value while storage surplus and weak seasonality odds offset supportive weather
summary: Front-month NG at $2.940 is essentially on top of today's model fair value ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417), so there is no immediate valuation edge. Seasonality is a mild headwind — price already ~10pp below its 16-year median-for-date, odds of being higher in 1/2/4 weeks are only 38%/38%/31% and the typical 4-week move is -3.1%, though the signal's own reliability is flagged Low this week (50pp 10-90% spread). Weather is the standout bullish input, with both the 1-7D and 8-16D demand outlooks running +8% to +13% above the 5-year normal, but it is colliding with a clearly bearish storage backdrop: 2,983 Bcf in storage, +184 Bcf above the 5-year average, and a +72 Bcf bearish surprise on the last build. Technicals show a persistent bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA on every timeframe from 5m to 1w) but several oversold readings (4h RSI 24.9, 1d closing below the lower Bollinger band) suggest the near-term downside is stretched. Managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range, leaving room for positioning to extend either way rather than signaling capitulation.

longTerm.rationale: Top-priority seasonality data leans mildly bearish (sub-50% odds of higher prices in 1/2/4 weeks, -3.1% typical 4-week move) and storage confirms a real fundamental surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y, +72 Bcf bearish surprise), but this is directly offset by above-normal weather demand in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows and a fair-value curve that is drifting higher (from $2.917 now to $3.040 by +21D). With price already sitting almost exactly on fair value and the seasonality signal itself flagged Low reliability, there is no clean directional edge yet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a rally into the $3.10-3.25 fair-value/resistance zone to sell into (with the bearish EMA trend still intact), or a confirmed breakdown below ~$2.75 alongside another above-forecast storage build, before sizing a directional position.

shortTerm.riskReward: approx 1:1.5 to TP2 (TP1 is a partial/scratch target)
shortTerm.rationale: Price is trading at fair value with no mispricing edge, but near-term weather demand is running +8.3% above the 5-year normal and technicals are stretched to the downside (4h RSI 24.9, 1d closing below the lower Bollinger band), favoring a short-term mean-reversion bounce. This is a counter-trend tactical trade against the broader 50<200 EMA downtrend, so conviction and size should stay small.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.80 or a fresh downside break in the 1-7D weather demand outlook would invalidate the bounce thesis.

Weak seasonality odds (31-38% chance of higher prices in 1/2/4 weeks) but explicitly flagged Low reliability this week
Weather demand running above 5y normal in both the 1-7D (+8.3%) and 8-16D (+12.7%) windows
Price essentially at fair value ($2.940 vs $2.917 fair, +$0.023 spread, status FAIR)
Bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA) across every timeframe from 5m to 1w, with short-term oversold readings (4h RSI 24.9, 1d below lower Bollinger band)
risks: Summer weather forecasts can flip quickly, reversing the demand tailwind that is currently the main bullish factor
